title: Reno Seniors EngAGED Program Update
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: January 21, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator
Cynthia Esparza, Director
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services Department
SUBJECT: Reno Seniors EngAGED Program Update
In April 2023, the Reno City Council allocated funds from the American Rescue Plan Act
(ARPA) for senior enrichment. The following is an overview of the free, senior-centered
programming offered by Reno Senior EngAGED from September to December 2025.
Date Event Participants
September – December 2025 Weekly Line Dance Classes 828
September - December 2025 Senior Art Classes 189
September – December 2025 Fun Friday Dances 210
October 9, & November 6, 2025 Senior Night at Dragon Lights 250
October 15, 2025 Senior Day at the Automobile Museum 124
October 23-24, 2025 Senior Tech Connect Classes 43
December 4, 2025 Adopt-a-Senior 50
December 11-12, 2025 Senior Tech Connect Classes 26
Total Participants: 1,720
ARPA funding for the Reno Seniors EngAGED Program will conclude by the end of December 2026. At present, no additional funds are available to extend the Reno Seniors EngAGED Program's programs beyond December 2026.

fields.authors: Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator; Cynthia Esparza, Director
fields.tldr: The Reno Seniors EngAGED Program provided free senior-centered programming from September to December 2025, serving a total of 1,720 participants across events including line dance classes, art classes, dances, and tech connect sessions. The program is funded through ARPA funds allocated by City Council in April 2023. ARPA funding will conclude by the end of December 2026, and no additional funds are currently available to extend the program beyond that date.
fields.key_stats: Total participants September–December 2025: 1,720; Weekly Line Dance Classes: 828 participants; Fun Friday Dances: 210 participants; Senior Art Classes: 189 participants; Senior Night at Dragon Lights: 250 participants; Senior Day at the Automobile Museum: 124 participants; Senior Tech Connect Classes (Oct): 43 participants; Adopt-a-Senior: 50 participants; Senior Tech Connect Classes (Dec): 26 participants.
fields.key_decisions: City Council allocated ARPA funds for senior enrichment in April 2023. No additional funds are currently available to extend the Reno Seniors EngAGED Program beyond December 2026.
fields.action_items: No specific action items are stated in the memo; however, the memo implicitly signals a need for future funding decisions before December 2026 to continue the program.

projects: American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) Senior Enrichment Funding
Senior Day at the Automobile Museum
